Politics.headline: Trump faces first big loss on Iran war
Politics.detail: Votes in the House and Senate to effectively halt the military campaign this week could deliver Democrats a rare victory and signal the war's widening unpopularity even among Republicans; however, President Trump is likely to veto any such legislation.

Politics.headline: Mass Protests Plunge Bolivia Into Political Upheaval
Politics.detail: Bolivia is experiencing significant political turmoil due to mass protests.

Politics.headline: Trump administration indicts Cuba's Raul Castro over 1996 plane shootdown
Politics.detail: The Trump administration has indicted Cuba's Raul Castro in connection with the 1996 shootdown of Brothers to the Rescue planes, marking a sharp escalation in tensions between Washington and Havana.

Politics.headline: China's Xi may visit North Korea as early as next week, Yonhap reports
Politics.detail: Chinese President Xi Jinping may visit North Korea as early as next week, according to South Korea's Yonhap news agency, potentially acting as a mediator between Trump and North Korean leader Kim Jong Un.

Politics.headline: Trump says he'll speak to Taiwan leader, calling democratic island a 'problem'
Politics.detail: President Donald Trump stated he would speak with Taiwan's leader, Lai Ching-te, and referred to the democratic island as a 'problem,' using similar language to China.

Technology.headline: SpaceX IPO Filing Offers First Glimpse at Starlink Subscriber Numbers, Financials
Technology.detail: SpaceX's IPO filing reveals details about Starlink's subscriber numbers and the company's financials, highlighting Elon Musk's focus on AI and transforming SpaceX into an AI powerhouse. Much of its outlook relies on SpaceX dominating technologies and markets that do not yet exist — from Mars missions to AI data centers in space.

Technology.headline: Valve Overhauls Steam Tags, Kills Off Dozens But Adds New Categories, Too
Technology.detail: Valve has updated Steam tags, removing some and introducing new categories to improve game discovery and organization on the platform. The overhaul aims to provide a more accurate and relevant tagging system for users browsing the Steam store.

Technology.headline: Advanced Technology International to Manage Leveraging Enterprise Technologies for Holistic AI, Logistics, Integration, Transformation, and Yield (LETHALITY) OTA
Technology.detail: Advanced Technology International (ATI) has been awarded an Other Transaction Agreement (OTA) by the Naval Surface Warfare Center, Corona Division (NSWCCO) to serve as the consortium manager for the Leveraging Enterprise Technologies for Holistic AI, Logistics, Integration, Transformation, and Yield (LETHALITY) consortium. The consortium aims to advance warfighting capabilities in areas like AI, cyber, and network architecture.

Technology.headline: Google Announces Slew of AI Advances, Including a Personal AI Assistant Coming Soon
Technology.detail: Google has unveiled a range of AI advancements, including a personal AI assistant, as part of its ongoing efforts to integrate AI more deeply into its products and services. The announcements highlight Google's commitment to pushing the boundaries of AI technology.

Technology.headline: Microsoft Is Ditching SMS 2FA Login Codes, Prioritizing Passkeys Instead
Technology.detail: Microsoft is moving away from SMS-based two-factor authentication (2FA) and focusing on passkeys as a more secure login method. This change aims to enhance user security by eliminating the vulnerabilities associated with SMS 2FA.

Science.headline: UK Beekeepers Aid Science with Honey Submissions
Science.detail: A study published in PLOS One reveals that beekeepers, acting as citizen scientists, collected honey samples that allowed researchers to identify over 800 plant species visited by honeybees, pinpointing commonly visited species like canola and clovers. This highlights the invaluable role of beekeepers in environmental surveys due to honeybees' ability to gather plant materials from wide areas.

Science.headline: Scientists Discover Brain Pathway That May Slow Parkinson's Disease – but Only in Women
Science.detail: A newly identified protective brain pathway may help preserve vulnerable neurons in Parkinson's disease, but the effect has been observed only in women. Further research is needed to understand the sex-specific mechanisms involved.

Science.headline: Immunotherapy could be used to treat depression, early trial suggests
Science.detail: An early trial suggests that immunotherapy, typically used to treat cancer, may be effective in treating depression. Further research is needed to confirm these findings and understand the underlying mechanisms.

Science.headline: SpaceX reveals plan for $1.75tn stock market debut that could make Musk a trillionaire
Science.detail: SpaceX has announced plans for a $1.75 trillion stock market debut, a move that could potentially make Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire. The debut is expected to generate significant interest and reshape the landscape of space exploration and technology investment.

Science.headline: Scientists Warn Many Insects May Not Survive a Warming World
Science.detail: A sweeping study of tropical insects reveals that many species may struggle to survive rising temperatures. This raises concerns about the future of biodiversity and the critical roles insects play in ecosystems.

Health.headline: DRC Ebola risk high regionally, low worldwide: WHO
Health.detail: The World Health Organization (WHO) has stated that the risk of the Ebola outbreak in central Africa is high regionally, but remains low globally, noting the virus has likely been spreading for months. The outbreak was declared in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo last Friday.

Health.headline: Ebola outbreak likely started months ago, spread undetected
Health.detail: WHO officials suggest the recent Ebola outbreak likely began months ago in central Africa and spread undetected until a super-spreader event in early May, potentially at a funeral. The rare Bundibugyo strain circulated undetected for a couple of months before being identified in blood samples on May 15.

Health.headline: US Health Secretary Kennedy Fires Heads Of Key Preventive Health Panel
Health.detail: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has fired the leaders of the U.S. Preventive Services Task Force, raising concerns he wants the group to endorse his personal views. The task force is an influential group of experts who decide which medical services insurers must cover under the Affordable Care Act.

Health.headline: Ontario Government Expands Access to Fertility Treatment
Health.detail: The Ontario government has expanded access to fertility treatment.

Health.headline: Sabiá virus has been circulating in Brazil for 142 years and mutating, study finds.
Health.detail: A study has found that the Sabiá virus has been circulating in Brazil for 142 years and undergoing mutations.

Sports.headline: Aaron Rodgers Says the 2026 NFL Season Will Be His Last: 'This Is It'
Sports.detail: Steelers QB Aaron Rodgers has announced that the 2026 NFL season will be his final one, marking the end of his two-decade career. He recently signed a one-year deal to return to the Steelers.

Sports.headline: Aston Villa Beat Freiburg in Istanbul to Win the Europa League
Sports.detail: Aston Villa secured the Europa League title by defeating Freiburg in Istanbul, marking a significant achievement and etching the names of a new generation of heroes in the club's history. Unai Emery led Villa to Europa League glory.

Sports.headline: Southampton Lose Spygate Appeal With Play-Off Expulsion Confirmed
Sports.detail: Southampton's appeal against their expulsion from the Championship play-offs due to a spying incident has been rejected. The EFL upheld the play-off ban for spying.

Sports.headline: NY Knicks Fans Chant 'We Want Wemby!' After Epic Comeback Win
Sports.detail: Following an impressive comeback victory, New York Knicks fans were heard chanting 'We Want Wemby!'. The fans are clearly excited about the future and potentially adding Victor Wembanyama to the team.

Sports.headline: Bubba Wallace 'Seeing Red' After Being Wrecked, Female Driver Rage-Quits and Cries & NASCAR Missed the Mark
Sports.detail: Bubba Wallace expressed his frustration after being involved in a wreck, while a female driver rage-quit and cried, leading to criticism of NASCAR. The incidents have sparked discussions about the sport's handling of such situations.

Entertainment.headline: Michael Jackson: The Verdict: Netflix announces release date for docuseries
Entertainment.detail: Netflix is releasing a docuseries focusing on Michael Jackson's trial, bringing the controversial case back into the spotlight. Stay updated with the latest entertainment news, OTT releases, movie releases, box office collection, celebrity buzz & global trends.

Entertainment.headline: Shownu and Hyungwon's more reserved Monsta X subunit still packs musical, emotional punch
Entertainment.detail: Shownu X Hyungwon, a subunit of the K-pop group Monsta X, is releasing their second EP, “Love Me,” which showcases a more reserved but still impactful musical style. This marks their first release in nearly three years, since their debut in 2023 with "The Unseen".

Entertainment.headline: Matthew Perry's 'Friends' scripts, Banksy artwork and belongings to be auctioned for charity
Entertainment.detail: Late actor Matthew Perry's belongings, including 'Friends' scripts and Banksy artwork, are set to be auctioned for charity. The auction aims to support causes aligned with Perry's values and legacy.

Entertainment.headline: North coast residency program announces successful artists
Entertainment.detail: Three artists from the Northern Rivers will visit the Clarence Valley in 2026 as part of the inaugural Northern Rivers Artist Exchange Residency Program (NRAE), a partnership between Grafton Regional Gallery and Arts Northern Rivers designed to foster creative connection, collaboration and.

Entertainment.headline: Leon Thomas to receive ASCAP Vanguard Award for shaping the future of R&B
Entertainment.detail: Leon Thomas is set to receive the ASCAP Vanguard Award for his contributions and influence in shaping the future of R&B music. The award recognizes his innovative work and impact on the genre.

Crime & Justice.headline: 3 killed in shooting at Islamic Center of San Diego, police say; 2 suspects dead
Crime & Justice.detail: Three people were killed in a shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego, and two suspected shooters were found dead inside a vehicle nearby, police said.

Crime & Justice.headline: 3 women charged in connection to 7-year-old girl's death on Long Island
Crime & Justice.detail: A woman, her mother and her daughter have all been charged in connection a child's death on Long Island.

Crime & Justice.headline: Saliva test to detect drugs is a game changer for DWI enforcement, police say
Crime & Justice.detail: Police on Long Island are now using a roadside saliva test that can detect marijuana use and other drugs in minutes.

Crime & Justice.headline: Questions about manhole complaints in NYC arise after woman falls to her death
Crime & Justice.detail: The shocking incident sparked questions about just how widespread manhole complaints are across New York City.

Crime & Justice.headline: Ex-prosecutor allegedly stole secret report on Trump, named file...
Crime & Justice.detail: An ex-prosecutor allegedly stole a secret report on Trump and named the file, according to CBS News.

Education.headline: CBSE Three-Language Policy Sparks Row In Karnataka, Students Voice Concerns
Education.detail: The implementation of the CBSE's three-language policy, making a third language mandatory for Class IX from July 1, has faced opposition from students in Karnataka. Concerns have been raised regarding the additional burden on students and the potential impact on their performance in other subjects.

Education.headline: THE Education Ministry is actively transitioning from inflexible examination-centric learning towards a more holistic path
Education.detail: The Education Ministry is shifting from exam-focused learning to a more holistic approach that values critical thinking, emotional intelligence, creativity, and diverse talents. However, schools and parents, particularly in traditional Chinese vernacular education, are resisting this transformation and still prefer hierarchical, obedience-focused methods.

Education.headline: STARTRADER CEO Peter Karsten Delivers 'AI Starter' Keynote at University of Adelaide
Education.detail: STARTRADER CEO Peter Karsten gave a keynote at the University of Adelaide, introducing AI concepts to students across disciplines. This reflects the company's dedication to AI and digital skills development for youth, highlighting the increasing importance of AI fluency in various professional fields.

Education.headline: Students make music for Summer Shake-Up
Education.detail: Marfa Live Arts is partnering with Marfa ISD and other organizations for the 2026 Summer Shake-Up, a playwriting program that incorporates music. Students will write their own play and perform it with local musicians, guided by Marfa Live Arts' lead instructor, Fatima Anaza.

Education.headline: Senators think they hold the key to a college athletics bill demanded by Trump
Education.detail: Senators Ted Cruz and Maria Cantwell are working on a college athletics bill to set new limits for how athletes can be paid, aiming to preempt the increasing difficulty of managing a patchwork of state laws. Cruz emphasized the importance of college sports for scholarships and education but noted the current unsustainable path due to the lack of consistent standards.

Lifestyle.headline: URBN Reports Record Q1 Sales and Income
Lifestyle.detail: Urban Outfitters, Inc. announced record net income of $115.7 million and earnings per diluted share of $1.30 for the three months ended April 30, 2026, with net sales increasing 11.4% to a record $1.48 billion. CEO Richard A. Hayne expressed confidence in URBN's continued success due to engaged customers responding to compelling fashion trends.

Lifestyle.headline: LG Electronics brings K-lifestyle showrooms to Southeast Asia to boost brand
Lifestyle.detail: LG Electronics is expanding customer experience spaces in Southeast Asia to allow overseas customers to experience LG products and lifestyle, aiming to enhance brand recognition. They also created an exhibition hall inspired by K-dramas at 'LG InnoFest 2026 APAC' in Busan.

Lifestyle.headline: Housewives vs working moms: Who faces more stress? A psychologist explains
Lifestyle.detail: A psychologist explains that both housewives and working mothers experience different forms of emotional, mental, and physical stress, each with its own set of health risks, emphasizing that chronic stress can quietly impact physical health if self-care is ignored. The discussion about who has a tougher life is more complex than it appears.

Lifestyle.headline: Why solo female travellers are flocking to these Indian destinations
Lifestyle.detail: Solo female travel in India is growing, with women exploring destinations known for their calm atmosphere and traveller-friendly setup, with places like McLeod Ganj, Shillong, and Hampi gaining attention for their safety and comfort. These destinations offer unique experiences ranging from mountain views and Tibetan culture to clean streets and historical ruins.

Lifestyle.headline: Posh Grandpa is fashion's new main character
Lifestyle.detail: The latest character dressing trend may be a little silly but there's an off-kilter pleasure in its mellow, vintage vibe. The trend is discussed by Jess Cartner-Morley on fashion.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: America is letting boys fail
Opinion/Editorial.detail: A Washington Post podcast discusses the challenges and societal factors contributing to boys falling behind in various aspects of life.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Congress Reviews Possible Changes to 2026 Refugee Admissions as Program Reaches Ceiling
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Ahead of Congressional consultations, the Trump administration is considering increasing the refugee admissions ceiling, potentially reserving slots for white Afrikaners from South Africa, a proposal condemned by Church World Service.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Anti-political dynasty bill nears House approval
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Philippine House of Representatives is close to passing the Anti-Political Dynasty law, which aims to prevent the concentration of political power within families.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Massie defeated: The Israel lobby's pyrrhic victory in Kentucky
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece on Al Jazeera discusses the implications of Thomas Massie's defeat in Kentucky and the role of the Israel lobby.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Only Taiwan can decide its future: Lai
Opinion/Editorial.detail: President William Lai emphasized that Taiwan's future should not be determined by external forces or held hostage by fear, division or short-term gain, marking the second anniversary of his inauguration.

Zimbabwe.headline: President urges youth to drive Zim's development and Vision 2030
Zimbabwe.detail: President Mnangagwa has called upon the youth to take the lead in Zimbabwe's development and the realization of Vision 2030. He emphasized the importance of their involvement in various sectors to achieve the country's goals.

Zimbabwe.headline: ConCourt on CAB3 challenge
Zimbabwe.detail: The Constitutional Court is deliberating on a challenge to Constitutional Amendment Bill (CAB) 3, which proposes changes to the presidential election process and term limits. The outcome could have significant implications for the upcoming elections.

Zimbabwe.headline: Local journalists experience digital-first media development model
Zimbabwe.detail: Local journalists are participating in a program focused on developing a digital-first media approach. This initiative aims to equip them with the skills and knowledge necessary to thrive in the evolving media landscape.

Zimbabwe.headline: 'wear traditional attire'. . . All set for Culture Month launch
Zimbabwe.detail: Preparations are complete for the launch of Culture Month, with a call for people to wear traditional attire. The event is expected to showcase Zimbabwe's rich cultural heritage and promote national unity.

Zimbabwe.headline: Colour, rhythm, unity Culture Month ignites Bulawayo
Zimbabwe.detail: Bulawayo is set to host a vibrant celebration of Culture Month, featuring colourful displays, rhythmic performances, and a spirit of unity. The commemorations will bring together people from across Zimbabwe to Barbourfields Stadium.
